story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a coercive recovery tool in individual contractual property disputes pending before the Debt Recovery Tribunal (DRT). The Court held that banks cannot invoke the IBC against a debtor for loans directly disbursed to a builder linked to construction progress, emphasizing that the IBC is not a shortcut for recovery.

story_clusters.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has arrested Punjab Industries Minister Sanjeev Arora in connection with an alleged money-laundering case involving fake GST invoices. Multiple reports indicate that the ED has accused him of using bogus invoices to claim GST refunds, with the alleged amount ranging from ₹100 crore to ₹157 crore. He has been sent to 7-day ED custody.

articles.title: IBC Cannot Be Invoked as a Coercive Recovery Tool in Individual Contractual Property Dispute Pending before DRT: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a coercive recovery tool in an individual contractual property dispute that is already pending before the Debt Recovery Tribunal (DRT). The Court stated that the case involves a contractual dispute, not a straightforward financial debt default, and the DRT is the appropriate forum for recovery. This ruling clarifies the scope of IBC's application and reinforces the jurisdiction of specialized tribunals in con
articles.raw_summary: The present case does not involve a straightforward financial debt-fault scenario warranting initiation of CIRP. The decision impacts how banks and financial institutions can pursue insolvency proceedings against borrowers in similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: The transaction cannot be viewed in isolation as a simple financial lending arrangement between the Bank and the CD, the Court said.

articles.title: RTE Act Mandate On Reservation Of Private School Seats For Poor Students Enforced? Supreme Court Asks States, UTs
articles.summary: The Supreme Court asked ten states and three Union Territories to explain whether they have implemented Section 12(1)(c) of the Right to Education (RTE) Act, 2009, which requires private schools to reserve 25% of seats for economically weaker sections. articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court recently asked ten States and three Union Territories to show whether they have implemented Section 12(1)(c) of the Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education(RTE) Act, 2009. On the failure to show the faithful implementation of Section 12(1)(c), the Court will be compelled to summon the Principal Secretaries of the Education Department of the said States/UTs. The present petition was filed in 2023 by one Md Imran Ahmad seeking the implementation of the said provision.

The ruling highlights the need for equitable maternity leave policies in government employment.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that courts must thoroughly examine plaintiffs' claims and reject lawsuits that indirectly seek to enforce benami (prohibited) transactions, even if drafted cleverly. The decision strengthens legal safeguards under Order VII Rule 11 of the Civil Procedure Code to prevent sham litigation. This ruling impacts financial policy enforcement and litigation involving benami transactions in India.

These demands were deemed 'totally unacceptable' by former US President Donald Trump. IBC Is Not a Recovery Shortcut: SC Rejects CIRP in Builder-Bank-Borrower Dispute
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that insolvency proceedings under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a shortcut to recover disputed dues from contractual transactions. The case involved a dispute between a builder, a bank, and a borrower. The court emphasized that the IBC is not a recovery mechanism but a process for resolving insolvency. This ruling clarifies the scope of the IBC and its limitations in commercial disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that insolvency proceedings under the IBC cannot be invoked merely to recover disputed dues arising from contractual transactions. The post IBC Is Not a Recovery Shortcut: SC Rejects CIRP in Builder-Bank-Borrower Dispute appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Govt land transfer code set up to pare disputes, boost monetisation
articles.summary: The central government has introduced a new nationwide framework to regulate transfers of central land. The policy aims to reduce disputes and boost revenue by mandating separate valuations for buildings, clear guidelines for public versus commercial use, and requiring cabinet approval for transfers to private entities or states. This is expected to enhance transparency and unlock funds for public projects.
articles.raw_summary: The Centre has introduced a nationwide framework for central government land transfers to curb disputes and boost asset monetisation. This new policy mandates separate valuations for buildings and clear guidelines for public versus commercial use, aiming to enhance transparency and unlock revenue for public projects. Transfers to private entities and states will now require cabinet approval.

articles.title: Entire Bank Deposits Cannot Be Taxed as Income – ITAT Restricts Addition to 2% in Renukamata Society Cases
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that entire bank deposits cannot automatically be treated as unexplained income in cases involving accommodation entry networks. The tribunal restricted taxable additions to just 2% of deposits after finding no evidence of actual financial gain by the assessee. The case involved Renukamata Society and highlights the need for evidence-based tax assessments rather than presumptive additions. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle simila
articles.raw_summary: The Pune ITAT held that entire cash deposits in bank accounts cannot automatically be treated as unexplained income when the assessee appears to be only a conduit in an accommodation entry network. The Tribunal restricted the taxable addition to 2% of deposits after finding no evidence of actual enrichment. The post Entire Bank Deposits Cannot Be Taxed as Income   ITAT Restricts Addition to 2% in Renukamata Society Cases appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court dismissed an FIR filed against a teacher for sharing an Urdu poem as a WhatsApp status. articles.raw_summary: ‘Sharing a poetic recitation without any additional commentary or intent to incite, cannot be construed as promoting enmity or public mischief,’ said the bench.

The criticism comes from exporters and industry stakeholders who argue for policy reforms to reduce this burden.

articles.title: Governor dissolves West Bengal assembly after completion of term
articles.summary: The West Bengal assembly was dissolved after completing its five-year term, as the current government under Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress was elected in May 2021. This is a routine constitutional process following the completion of the assembly's term. The dissolution paves the way for new elections to constitute the next legislative body.

This decision impacts how excise duty is calculated on commercial transactions involving bulk discounts.

articles.title: Section 271(1)(c) Penalty Quashed as Wrong Claim Alone Is Not Concealment: ITAT Rajkot
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Rajkot quashed a penalty imposed under Section 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act, ruling that a mere disallowance of expenditure during assessment does not automatically constitute concealment or furnishing of inaccurate particulars. The assessee had claimed a wrong deduction, but the tribunal found no intent to evade tax. This decision sets a precedent for future tax penalty cases in India.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Rajkot held that mere disallowance of expenditure during assessment proceedings does not automatically justify penalty under Section 271(1)(c). The Tribunal found no concealment or furnishing of inaccurate particulars by the assessee. articles.title: RBI officers seek governor's intervention to correct promotion policy
articles.summary: RBI officers are protesting recent changes to the promotion policy. The Reserve Bank of India Officers' Association has written to the Governor seeking intervention. The new policy links promotions to vacancy availability, replacing the earlier time-bound system. This has caused widespread dissatisfaction and demoralization among officers. The association demands an immediate review and the reintroduction of assured, time-bound promotions.

articles.title: States told to shift to new base year for GSDP by end of FY27
articles.summary: The statistics ministry has instructed all states to adopt 2022-23 as the new base year for calculating Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) by the end of fiscal year 2027. This change aims to align state-level economic data with the revised national GDP framework, improving accuracy. States are also permitted to develop sub-state Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures, though no central directive mandates this.
articles.raw_summary: The statistics ministry has directed states to adopt 2022-23 as the new base year for Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) computation by fiscal 2027. This move aligns state-level estimates with the revised national GDP framework, enhancing regional economic data accuracy. While states are also free to develop sub-state CPI, there's no central directive for this.

articles.title: No Addition, No Under-Reporting: ITAT Deletes Section 270A Penalty When Income Returned u/s 148 Was Fully Accepted
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that no penalty for under-reporting income can be imposed if the Assessing Officer accepts the income declared in response to a Section 148 notice without any additions. The Tribunal deleted the entire penalty under Section 270A in this case. This decision clarifies the legal interpretation of tax penalties and reinforces the principle that penalties require actual under-reporting, not just a reassessment.
articles.raw_summary: Pune ITAT held that penalty for under-reporting of income cannot survive where the Assessing Officer accepts the income declared in response to notice under Section 148 without any addition. The Tribunal deleted the entire penalty under Section 270A. The post No Addition, No Under-Reporting: ITAT Deletes Section 270A Penalty When Income Returned u/s 148 Was Fully Accepted appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Section 50C Addition as Stamp Value Difference Was Within 5% Safe Harbour
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Indore ruled that Section 50C of the Income Tax Act cannot be applied when the difference between the actual sale price of a property and its stamp duty valuation is within 5%. In this case, the difference was only 1.19%, so the tribunal directed the use of the actual sale consideration for calculating capital gains. This decision clarifies the application of tax rules for property transactions and sets a precedent for similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Indore held that Section 50C could not be invoked where the difference between actual sale consideration and stamp duty valuation was only 1.19%. The Tribunal directed adoption of actual sale consideration as the full value for capital gains computation. The post ITAT Deletes Section 50C Addition as Stamp Value Difference Was Within 5% Safe Harbour appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Freebie burden may strain state finances, push borrowing higher: Economists
articles.summary: Economists warn that rising welfare spending by Indian states, including subsidies and freebies, could strain state finances and force cuts to capital expenditure. M R Madhavan, Co-founder of PRS Legislative Research, highlights that a large portion of state revenues is already committed to salaries, pensions, interest payments, and subsidies, leaving little room for discretionary spending. He notes this could ultimately push states to borrow more.
articles.raw_summary: M R Madhavan, Co-founder & Pres, PRS Legislative Research, and who has previously worked as an economist with I-Sec, ICICI and Bank of America, said states may ultimately be forced to squeeze capital expenditure if welfare spending rises while revenues disappoint. He noted that a large portion of state revenues is already committed towards salaries, pensions, interest payments and subsidies, leaving limited room for discretionary spending.

articles.title: Aggregators to be charged interest at 12% per annum if they fail to contribute toward social security of their gig workers
articles.summary: The government has mandated that aggregators (platforms like food delivery or ride-hailing services) must contribute to the social security of gig workers or face a 12% annual interest penalty on delayed payments. All aggregators must upload gig worker details to a central portal within 45 days, with real-time updates for new appointments or exits. These rules aim to ensure gig workers receive timely social security benefits.
articles.raw_summary: Aggregators now face a 12% annual interest penalty for delayed social security contributions for gig workers. All aggregators must upload gig worker details to a central portal within 45 days. New appointments and exits require real-time or daily registration. These rules ensure gig workers receive social security benefits.

This dispute highlights tensions over the governor's discretion in government formation and the opposition's demand for a procedural resolution.

articles.title: Non-Publication Of Marks Of Unsuccessful Candidates Wouldn't Raise An Inference That They Passed Examination : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the non-publication of marks for unsuccessful candidates in an examination does not imply they passed. The Court clarified that this practice cannot grant them a right to appointment unless explicitly required by recruitment rules or advertisements. The ruling addresses legal ambiguities in examination and recruitment procedures.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that non-disclosure of marks cannot by itself confer a right to appointment where the recruitment rules or advertisement do not require publication of marks.

The article explores how cultural and religious norms shape environmental protection in the region. Such community-led initiatives may inform broader environmental policy discussions in India.

articles.title: NCLT Rejects Confidentiality Breach Plea as Email to Bidder Was Found Inadvertent
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) rejected a plea alleging a confidentiality breach after an email containing sensitive information was inadvertently sent to a prospective bidder due to an auto-suggest error. The Tribunal found no malicious intent or unfair advantage taken by the recipient. The ruling clarifies that such isolated errors do not constitute deliberate breaches of confidentiality under corporate governance norms.

articles.title: Welfare spending surge pushes subsidy outgo to 91% of target
articles.summary: The Indian government's welfare spending has surged, with food subsidies reaching ₹1.92 trillion (85% of the revised target) and urea subsidies exceeding the annual target at ₹1.28 trillion (101% of revised estimates). Urea subsidy outgo is also higher than the previous year's ₹1.16 trillion. This reflects increased expenditure on social welfare programs, particularly food and agricultural subsidies, which may prompt policy reviews or adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Food subsidy, the largest component, stood at ₹1.92 trillion, or 85% of the revised estimate of ₹2.28 trillion. However, the urea subsidy has already breached its annual target, reaching ₹1.28 trillion, or 101% of the revised estimates, and higher than the ₹1.16 trillion recorded a year ago.

articles.title: Gig workers must work 90 days a year for social security benefits
articles.summary: The government has introduced new rules under the Code on Social Security requiring gig and platform workers to work at least 90 days a year with a single aggregator to qualify for social security benefits. articles.title: S. 25 Hindu Succession Act | Murder Accused Cannot Claim Inheritance Of Murdered Person's Property : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a person accused of murder cannot inherit the property of the victim, even without a formal conviction. The Court clarified that the disqualification applies to murder-accused individuals under Section 25 of the Hindu Succession Act. This decision reinforces legal safeguards against unjust inheritance claims by those involved in criminal acts.
articles.raw_summary: The Court clarified that an actual conviction is not necessary, and a murder-accused will also be disqualified from inheritance.

articles.title: Vijay, MGR, NTR: How film fame became political power in the South
articles.summary: South India has witnessed a unique political culture where cinematic fame often transforms into real political influence. From legends like MGR and N T Rama Rao to contemporary figures like Vijay, film personalities have repeatedly leveraged mass appeal, fan bases, and cultural identity to enter politics. This long-standing phenomenon continues to shape electoral outcomes and redefine leadership in Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and beyond.

articles.title: Services, retail lending drives banks’ credit growth to nearly 16% in FY26
articles.summary: India's banking sector saw credit growth of nearly 16% in FY26, driven by strong retail and services lending. Personal loans grew 16.2%, supported by vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit remained stable. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) may monitor these trends for potential policy adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Retail lending remained strong, with personal loans rising 16.2%, up from 11.7% a year earlier, supported by demand for vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit growth remained steady. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs delays Fed cut outlook to December 2026 as Iran war drives US inflation
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs has pushed back its forecast for U.S. Federal Reserve interest rate cuts to December 2026 and March 2027, citing persistent inflation driven by rising energy prices from the Middle East conflict. The delay reflects concerns that elevated fuel costs could keep inflation above the Fed's target. This outlook may influence global financial markets and economic conditions, including those relevant to India.
articles.raw_summary: Goldman Sachs now anticipates U.S. Federal Reserve rate cuts in December 2026 and March 2027, delaying its previous September and December 2024 outlook. Elevated energy prices, driven by the Middle East conflict, are expected to maintain inflation above the Fed's target, prompting caution among policymakers.

articles.title: When Heritage Becomes a Market Category, Proof Must Follow the Launch
articles.summary: The Padma Doree initiative has launched a premium fabric line combining Eri silk and Chanderi, positioning itself as a sustainable and artisan-focused category. The initiative claims to prioritize sustainability and artisan value but lacks transparency on earnings distribution, decision-making, risk-sharing, and market demand durability. The article questions whether the premium benefits loom workers and calls for proof of sustainable impact. The initiative involves artisans and textile craftsma
articles.raw_summary: The just-launched Padma Doree initiative has been positioned as more than a fabric line, with Eri silk and Chanderi brought together inside a premium craft category that speaks of sustainability and artisan value, yet the claim will remain incomplete until the initiative shows who earns, who decides, who carries risk and whether the premium travels back to the loom through durable market demand.

articles.title: Courts must not apply SC/ST Act bail bar mechanically: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that courts must not mechanically apply bail restrictions under the SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act. The order is significant for cases where allegations under this Act are combined with general criminal offenses. The ruling aims to prevent arbitrary denial of bail in such cases.

articles.title: TMC appoints Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay LoP after losing West Bengal
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) appointed Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay as the Leader of the Opposition (LoP) in West Bengal. articles.title: Das Adam Smith Problem: rethinking Smith’s moral and economic worlds
articles.summary: The article explores the 'Das Adam Smith Problem,' which highlights a perceived contradiction between sympathy and self-interest in Adam Smith's writings. Scholars argue that his philosophy actually forms a coherent whole, uniting ethics and economics. The piece is an opinion analysis from The Hindu National and does not relate to Indian policy matters.
articles.raw_summary: The Das Adam Smith Problem shows an apparent dichotomy between sympathy and self-interest in Adam Smith’s works, but most scholars now view it as a misunderstanding, arguing that his philosophy forms a coherent whole uniting ethics and economics

articles.title: Defying China ‘pressure’, Taiwan president completes surprise Eswatini visit, is escorted home by F-16s
articles.summary: Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te completed a surprise visit to Eswatini, an African nation, despite China reportedly pressuring other countries to deny airspace permissions for his aircraft. The visit was conducted under tight security, with Lai escorted home by Taiwanese F-16 fighter jets. The incident highlights ongoing tensions between Taiwan and China, which could influence India's diplomatic and security strategies in the region.
articles.raw_summary: The visit took place days after Taiwan said a planned trip was disrupted because China pressured other African nations to revoke airspace permissions for Lai's aircraft.

articles.title: Kaziranga hosts a healthy population of fishing cats, finds survey
articles.summary: A first-of-its-kind scientific survey in Kaziranga National Park and Tiger Reserve has identified 57 fishing cats, confirming their widespread presence and active reproduction in the reserve’s floodplains. Conducted by Kaziranga’s Tiger Cell in collaboration with the Fishing Cat Project, the survey underscores the park’s role in supporting threatened wildlife. The findings could inform conservation policies or habitat protection measures for endangered species.
articles.raw_summary: The first scientific survey of fishing cats (Prionailurus viverrinus) in Kaziranga National Park and Tiger Reserve has recorded 57 individuals, confirming that the species is widely distributed and actively reproducing across the reserve’s floodplains. Conducted by Kaziranga’s Tiger Cell in coordination with the Fishing Cat Project, the survey report was released on February 22 – [ ]

The incident raises concerns about the enforcement of laws protecting ancient monuments and the rights of vulnerable groups affected by such actions.

articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government has shifted electricity connections from prepaid to postpaid billing for consumers with smart meters. The move is part of broader efforts to modernize the state's power distribution system and improve billing transparency.

This decision highlights the need for policy reforms to address gaps in disability rights protections.

articles.summary: Social media platforms are now required to remove non-consensual intimate images and deepfakes within two hours of complaints, a significant improvement from the previous 24-hour deadline under updated IT rules. Workers and businesses are bearing the brunt of these supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: Vijay's TVK warns all MLAs will resign if DMK and AIADMK join hands for Tamil Nadu govt formation
articles.summary: Vijay's party TVK has warned that all 107 of its MLAs may resign if the DMK and AIADMK join hands to form the Tamil Nadu government.
